title.title: Learning Objectives
title.subtitle: What you will learn in this course

heading: By the End of This Course
intro: You will be able to compare, evaluate, and select blockchain platforms based on their architecture, consensus models, and real-world trade-offs.

bitcoin.title: Bitcoin: The First Permissionless Blockchain
bitcoin.description: Map Bitcoin's four-layer architecture, explain the UTXO transaction model, understand Proof of Work and the halving schedule, and analyze Bitcoin's trilemma trade-offs.

ethereum.title: Ethereum: Programmable Blockchain
ethereum.description: Explain Ethereum accounts, the EVM execution model, smart contract deployment, and the Proof of Work → Proof of Stake transition and its implications.

hyperledger.title: Permissioned Blockchains: Hyperledger Fabric
hyperledger.description: Understand why enterprises choose permissioned blockchains, how channels and endorsement policies work, and where Hyperledger Fabric fits in the supply chain.

comparing.title: Comparing Platforms & the Trilemma
comparing.description: Apply the Blockchain Trilemma framework to compare Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Hyperledger — identifying which properties each platform prioritizes and sacrifices.

interoperability.title: Interoperability & Emerging Trends
interoperability.description: Explore cross-chain communication protocols (Cosmos, IBC), Layer 0 architectures, ZK-rollup platforms like Starknet, and the future of blockchain interoperability.
